NKJV.1: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
NKJV.2: And when He had fasted forty days and forty nights, afterward He was hungry.
NKJV.3: Now when the tempter came to Him, he said, “If You are the Son of God, command that these stones become bread.”
NKJV.4: But He answered and said, “It is written,  ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds from the mouth of God.’ ”
NKJV.5: Then the devil took Him up into the holy city, set Him on the pinnacle of the temple,
NKJV.6: and said to Him, “If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down. For it is written: ‘He shall give His angels charge over you,’ and, ‘In their hands they shall bear you up, Lest you dash your foot against a stone.’ ”
NKJV.7: Jesus said to him, “It is written again,  ‘You shall not tempt the LORD your God.’ ”
NKJV.8: Again, the devil took Him up on an exceedingly high mountain, and showed Him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
NKJV.9: And he said to Him, “All these things I will give You if You will fall down and worship me.”
NKJV.10: Then Jesus said to him, “Away with you, Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the LORD your God, and Him only you shall serve.’ ”
NKJV.11: Then the devil left Him, and behold, angels came and ministered to Him.
NKJV.12: Now when Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, He departed to Galilee.
NKJV.13: And leaving Nazareth, He came and dwelt in Capernaum, which is by the sea, in the regions of Zebulun and Naphtali,
NKJV.14: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Isaiah the prophet, saying:
NKJV.15: “The  land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, By the way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles:
NKJV.16: The people who sat in darkness have seen a great light, And upon those who sat in the region and shadow of death Light has dawned.”
NKJV.17: From that time Jesus began to preach and to say, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”
NKJV.18: And Jesus, walking by the Sea of Galilee, saw two brothers, Simon called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea; for they were fishermen.
NKJV.19: Then He said to them, “Follow Me, and  I will make you fishers of men.”
NKJV.20: They immediately left their nets and followed Him.
NKJV.21: Going on from there, He saw two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets. He called them,
NKJV.22: and immediately they left the boat and their father, and followed Him.
NKJV.23: And Jesus went about all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing all kinds of sickness and all kinds of disease among the people.
NKJV.24: Then His fame went throughout all Syria; and they brought to Him all sick people who were afflicted with various diseases and torments, and those who were demon-possessed, epileptics, and paralytics; and He healed them.
NKJV.25: Great multitudes followed Him—from Galilee, and from Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and beyond the Jordan.

ESV.1: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
ESV.2: And after fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry.
ESV.3: And the tempter came and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command these stones to become loaves of bread.”
ESV.4: But he answered,  “It is written,  “‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God.’”
ESV.5: Then the devil took him to the holy city and set him on the pinnacle of the temple
ESV.6: and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down, for it is written, “‘He will command his angels concerning you,’  and “‘On their hands they will bear you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.’”
ESV.7: Jesus said to him, “Again it is written, ‘You shall not put the Lord your God to the test.’”
ESV.8: Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
ESV.9: And he said to him, “All these I will give you, if you will fall down and worship me.”
ESV.10: Then Jesus said to him, “Be gone, Satan! For it is written,  “‘You shall worship the Lord your God and him only shall you serve.’”
ESV.11: Then the devil left him, and behold, angels came and were ministering to him.
ESV.12: Now when he heard that John had been arrested, he withdrew into Galilee.
ESV.13: And leaving Nazareth he went and lived in Capernaum by the sea, in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali,
ESV.14: so that what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah might be fulfilled:
ESV.15: “The land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, the way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles —
ESV.16: the people dwelling in darkness have seen a great light, and for those dwelling in the region and shadow of death, on them a light has dawned.”
ESV.17: From that time Jesus began to preach, saying,  “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”
ESV.18: While walking by the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon (who is called Peter) and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.
ESV.19: And he said to them, “Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.”
ESV.20: Immediately they left their nets and followed him.
ESV.21: And going on from there he saw two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets, and he called them.
ESV.22: Immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
ESV.23: And he went throughout all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom and healing every disease and every affliction among the people.
ESV.24: So his fame spread throughout all Syria, and they brought him all the sick, those afflicted with various diseases and pains, those oppressed by demons, those having seizures, and paralytics, and he healed them.
ESV.25: And great crowds followed him from Galilee and the Decapolis, and from Jerusalem and Judea, and from beyond the Jordan.

NASB.1: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
NASB.2: And after He had fasted forty days and forty nights, He then became hungry.
NASB.3: And the tempter came and said to Him, 'If You are the Son of God, command that these stones become bread.'
NASB.4: But He answered and said, 'It is written, 'MAN SHALL NOT LIVE ON BREAD ALONE, BUT ON EVERY WORD THAT PROCEEDS OUT OF THE MOUTH OF GOD.''
NASB.5: Then the devil took Him into the holy city and had Him stand on the pinnacle of the temple,
NASB.6: and said to Him, 'If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down; for it is written, 'HE WILL COMMAND HIS ANGELS CONCERNING YOU'; and 'ON [their] HANDS THEY WILL BEAR YOU UP, SO THAT YOU WILL NOT STRIKE YOUR FOOT AGAINST A STONE.''
NASB.7: Jesus said to him, 'On the other hand, it is written, 'YOU SHALL NOT PUT THE LORD YOUR GOD TO THE TEST.''
NASB.8: Again, the devil took Him to a very high mountain and showed Him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory;
NASB.9: and he said to Him, 'All these things I will give You, if You fall down and worship me.'
NASB.10: Then Jesus said to him, 'Go, Satan! For it is written, 'YOU SHALL WORSHIP THE LORD YOUR GOD, AND SERVE HIM ONLY.''
NASB.11: Then the devil left Him; and behold, angels came and [began] to minister to Him.
NASB.12: Now when Jesus heard that John had been taken into custody, He withdrew into Galilee;
NASB.13: and leaving Nazareth, He came and settled in Capernaum, which is by the sea, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali.
NASB.14: [This was] to fulfill what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet:
NASB.15: 'THE LAND OF ZEBULUN AND THE LAND OF NAPHTALI, BY THE WAY OF THE SEA, BEYOND THE JORDAN, GALILEE OF THE GENTILES--
NASB.16: 'THE PEOPLE WHO WERE SITTING IN DARKNESS SAW A GREAT LIGHT, AND THOSE WHO WERE SITTING IN THE LAND AND SHADOW OF DEATH, UPON THEM A LIGHT DAWNED.'
NASB.17: From that time Jesus began to preach and say, 'Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.'
NASB.18: Now as Jesus was walking by the Sea of Galilee, He saw two brothers, Simon who was called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea; for they were fishermen.
NASB.19: And He said to them, 'Follow Me, and I will make you fishers of men.'
NASB.20: Immediately they left their nets and followed Him.
NASB.21: Going on from there He saw two other brothers, James the [son] of Zebedee, and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets; and He called them.
NASB.22: Immediately they left the boat and their father, and followed Him.
NASB.23: Jesus was going throughout all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every kind of disease and every kind of sickness among the people.
NASB.24: The news about Him spread throughout all Syria; and they brought to Him all who were ill, those suffering with various diseases and pains, demoniacs, epileptics, paralytics; and He healed them.
NASB.25: Large crowds followed Him from Galilee and [the] Decapolis and Jerusalem and Judea and [from] beyond the Jordan.

NLT.1: Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted there by the devil.
NLT.2: For forty days and forty nights he fasted and became very hungry.
NLT.3: During that time the devil came and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become loaves of bread.”
NLT.4:
But Jesus told him, “No! The Scriptures say, ‘People do not live by bread alone,
but by every word that comes from the mouth of God.’ ”
NLT.5: Then the devil took him to the holy city, Jerusalem, to the highest point of the Temple,
NLT.6:
and said, “If you are the Son of God, jump off! For the Scriptures say, ‘He will order his angels to protect you.
And they will hold you up with their hands
so you won’t even hurt your foot on a stone.’ ”
NLT.7: Jesus responded, “The Scriptures also say, ‘You must not test the LORD your God.’ ”
NLT.8: Next the devil took him to the peak of a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
NLT.9: “I will give it all to you,” he said, “if you will kneel down and worship me.”
NLT.10:
“Get out of here, Satan,” Jesus told him. “For the Scriptures say, ‘You must worship the LORD your God
and serve only him.’ ”
NLT.11: Then the devil went away, and angels came and took care of Jesus.
NLT.12: When Jesus heard that John had been arrested, he left Judea and returned to Galilee.
NLT.13: He went first to Nazareth, then left there and moved to Capernaum, beside the Sea of Galilee, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali.
NLT.14: This fulfilled what God said through the prophet Isaiah:
NLT.15:
“In the land of Zebulun and of Naphtali,
beside the sea, beyond the Jordan River,
in Galilee where so many Gentiles live,
NLT.16:
the people who sat in darkness
have seen a great light.
And for those who lived in the land where death casts its shadow,
a light has shined.”
NLT.17: From then on Jesus began to preach, “Repent of your sins and turn to God, for the Kingdom of Heaven is near. ”
NLT.18: One day as Jesus was walking along the shore of the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers — Simon, also called Peter, and Andrew — throwing a net into the water, for they fished for a living.
NLT.19: Jesus called out to them, “Come, follow me, and I will show you how to fish for people!”
NLT.20: And they left their nets at once and followed him.
NLT.21: A little farther up the shore he saw two other brothers, James and John, sitting in a boat with their father, Zebedee, repairing their nets. And he called them to come, too.
NLT.22: They immediately followed him, leaving the boat and their father behind.
NLT.23: Jesus traveled throughout the region of Galilee, teaching in the synagogues and announcing the Good News about the Kingdom. And he healed every kind of disease and illness.
NLT.24: News about him spread as far as Syria, and people soon began bringing to him all who were sick. And whatever their sickness or disease, or if they were demon possessed or epileptic or paralyzed — he healed them all.
NLT.25: Large crowds followed him wherever he went — people from Galilee, the Ten Towns, Jerusalem, from all over Judea, and from east of the Jordan River.

WEB.1: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
WEB.2: When he had fasted forty days and forty nights, he was hungry afterward.
WEB.3: The tempter came and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command that these stones become bread.”
WEB.4: But he answered, “It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds out of the mouth of God.’”
WEB.5: Then the devil took him into the holy city. He set him on the pinnacle of the temple,
WEB.6: and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down, for it is written, ‘He will put his angels in charge of you.’ and, ‘On their hands they will bear you up, so that you don’t dash your foot against a stone.’”
WEB.7: Jesus said to him, “Again, it is written, ‘You shall not test the Lord, your God.’”
WEB.8: Again, the devil took him to an exceedingly high mountain, and showed him all the kingdoms of the world, and their glory.
WEB.9: He said to him, “I will give you all of these things, if you will fall down and worship me.”
WEB.10: Then Jesus said to him, “Get behind me,  Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the Lord your God, and you shall serve him only.’”
WEB.11: Then the devil left him, and behold, angels came and served him.
WEB.12: Now when Jesus heard that John was delivered up, he withdrew into Galilee.
WEB.13: Leaving Nazareth, he came and lived in Capernaum, which is by the sea, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali,
WEB.14: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,
WEB.15: “The land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, toward the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles,
WEB.16: the people who sat in darkness saw a great light, to those who sat in the region and shadow of death, to them light has dawned.”
WEB.17: From that time, Jesus began to preach, and to say, “Repent! For the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand.”
WEB.18: Walking by the sea of Galilee, he  saw two brothers: Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, his brother, casting a net into the sea; for they were fishermen.
WEB.19: He said to them, “Come after me, and I will make you fishers for men.”
WEB.20: They immediately left their nets and followed him.
WEB.21: Going on from there, he saw two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets. He called them.
WEB.22: They immediately left the boat and their father, and followed him.
WEB.23: Jesus went about in all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the Good News of the Kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness among the people.
WEB.24: The report about him went out into all Syria. They brought to him all who were sick, afflicted with various diseases and torments, possessed with demons, epileptics, and paralytics; and he healed them.
WEB.25: Great multitudes from Galilee, Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea and from beyond the Jordan followed him.

CSB17.1: Then <sup>ⓑ</sup> Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.2: After he had fasted forty days and forty nights, <sup>ⓓ</sup> he was hungry.
CSB17.3: Then the tempter approached him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.” <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.4: He answered, “It is written: Man must not live on bread alone but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.” [1]
CSB17.5: Then the devil took him to the holy city, had him stand on the pinnacle of the temple, <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.6:
and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down. For it is written: He will give his angels ⓖ orders concerning you,
and they will support you with their hands
so that you will not strike
your foot against a stone.” [2] ⓗ
CSB17.7: Jesus told him, “It is also written: Do not test the Lord your God.” [3] ⓘ
CSB17.8: Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor.
CSB17.9: And he said to him, “I will give you all these things if you will fall down and worship me.” <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.10: Then Jesus told him, “Go away, [5] Satan! For it is written: Worship the Lord your God, and serve only him.” [6] ⓙ
CSB17.11: Then the devil left him, and angels came and began to serve him. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.12: When he heard that John had been arrested, <sup>ⓛ</sup> he withdrew into Galilee. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.13: He left Nazareth and went to live in Capernaum <sup>ⓝ</sup> by the sea, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali.
CSB17.14: This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:
CSB17.15:
Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali,
along the road by the sea, beyond the Jordan,
Galilee of the Gentiles.
CSB17.16:
The people who live in darkness
have seen a great light,
and for those living in the land of the shadow of death,
a light has dawned. [7] [8] ⓐ
CSB17.17: From then on Jesus began to preach, “Repent, because the kingdom of heaven <sup>ⓑ</sup> has come near.”
CSB17.18: As <sup>ⓒ</sup> he was walking along the Sea of Galilee, <sup>ⓓ</sup> he saw two brothers, Simon (who is called Peter), and his brother Andrew. They were casting a net into the sea — for they were fishermen.
CSB17.19: “Follow me,” he told them, “and I will make you fish for  <sup>[9]</sup> people.”
CSB17.20: Immediately they left their nets and followed him.
CSB17.21: Going on from there, he saw two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee, and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father, preparing their nets, and he called them.
CSB17.22: Immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
CSB17.23: Now Jesus began to go all over Galilee, <sup>ⓔ</sup> teaching in their synagogues, preaching the good news of the kingdom, and healing every <sup>[10]</sup> disease and sickness <sup>[11]</sup> among the people. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.24: Then the news about him spread throughout Syria. <sup>ⓖ</sup> So they brought to him all those who were afflicted, those suffering from various diseases and intense pains, the demon-possessed, the epileptics, and the paralytics. <sup>ⓗ</sup> And he healed them.
CSB17.25: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and beyond the Jordan. <sup>ⓘ</sup>

AMP.1: Then Jesus was led by the [Holy] Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
AMP.2: After He had gone without food for forty days and forty nights, He became hungry.
AMP.3: And the tempter came and said to Him, "If You are the Son of God, command that these stones become bread."
AMP.4: But Jesus replied, "It is written and forever remains written, 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes out of the mouth of God.'"
AMP.5: Then the devil took Him into the holy city [Jerusalem] and placed Him on the pinnacle (highest point) of the temple.
AMP.6:
And he said [mockingly] to Him, "If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down; for it is written,
'He will command His angels concerning You [to serve, care for, protect and watch over You]';
and
'They will lift you up on their hands,
So that You will not strike Your foot against a stone.'"
AMP.7: Jesus said to him, "On the other hand, it is written and forever remains written, 'You shall not test the Lord your God.'"
AMP.8: Again, the devil took Him up on a very high mountain and showed Him all the kingdoms of the world and the glory [splendor, magnificence, and excellence] of them;
AMP.9: and he said to Him, "All these things I will give You, if You fall down and worship me."
AMP.10: Then Jesus said to him, "Go away, Satan! For it is written and forever remains written, 'You shall worship the Lord your God, and serve Him only.'"
AMP.11: Then the devil left Him; and angels came and ministered to Him [bringing Him food and serving Him].
AMP.12: Now when Jesus heard that John [the Baptist] had been arrested and put in prison, He left for Galilee.
AMP.13: And leaving Nazareth, He went and settled in Capernaum, which is by the sea, in the country of Zebulun and Naphtali.
AMP.14: This was to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah:
AMP.15:
"The land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali,
By the way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee [in the district] of the Gentiles—
AMP.16:
"The people who were sitting (living) in [spiritual] darkness have seen a great Light,
And for those who were sitting (living) in the land and shadow of [spiritual and moral] death,
Upon them a Light has dawned."
AMP.17: From that time Jesus began to preach and say, " Repent [change your inner self—your old way of thinking, regret past sins, live your life in a way that proves repentance; seek God's purpose for your life], for the kingdom of heaven is at hand."
AMP.18: As Jesus was walking by the Sea of Galilee, He noticed two brothers, Simon who was called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea; for they were fishermen.
AMP.19: And He said to them, " Follow Me [as My disciples, accepting Me as your Master and Teacher and walking the same path of life that I walk], and I will make you fishers of men."
AMP.20: Immediately they left their nets and followed Him [becoming His disciples, believing and trusting in Him and following His example].
AMP.21: And going on [further] from there He noticed two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee, and his brother John, in the boat with their father Zebedee, mending their nets; and He called them [to follow Him as His disciples].
AMP.22: Immediately they left the boat and their father, and followed Him [becoming His disciples, believing and trusting in Him and following His example].
AMP.23: And He went throughout all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news (gospel) of the kingdom, and healing every kind of disease and every kind of sickness among the people [demonstrating and revealing that He was indeed the promised Messiah].
AMP.24: So the news about Him spread throughout all Syria; and they brought to Him all who were sick, those suffering with various diseases and pains, those under the power of demons, and epileptics, paralytics; and He healed them.
AMP.25: Large crowds followed Him from Galilee and the Decapolis and Jerusalem and Judea and the other side of the Jordan.

MSG.1: Next Jesus was taken into the wild by the Spirit for the Test. The Devil was ready to give it.
MSG.2: Jesus prepared for the Test by fasting forty days and forty nights. That left him, of course, in a state of extreme hunger,
MSG.3: which the Devil took advantage of in the first test: "Since you are God's Son, speak the word that will turn these stones into loaves of bread."
MSG.4: Jesus answered by quoting Deuteronomy: "It takes more than bread to stay alive. It takes a steady stream of words from God's mouth."
MSG.5: For the second test the Devil took him to the Holy City. He sat him on top of the Temple and said,
MSG.7: Jesus countered with another citation from Deuteronomy: "Don't you dare test the Lord your God."
MSG.8: For the third test, the Devil took him on the peak of a huge mountain. He gestured expansively, pointing out all the earth's kingdoms, how glorious they all were.
MSG.9: Then he said, "They're yours—lock, stock, and barrel. Just go down on your knees and worship me, and they're yours."
MSG.10: Jesus' refusal was curt: "Beat it, Satan!" He backed his rebuke with a third quotation from Deuteronomy: "Worship the Lord your God, and only him. Serve him with absolute single-heartedness."
MSG.11: The Test was over. The Devil left. And in his place, angels! Angels came and took care of Jesus' needs.
MSG.12: When Jesus got word that John had been arrested, he returned to Galilee.
MSG.13: He moved from his hometown, Nazareth, to the lakeside village Capernaum, nestled at the base of the Zebulun and Naphtali hills.
MSG.15: Land of Zebulun, land of Naphtali, road to the sea, over Jordan, Galilee, crossroads for the nations.
MSG.16: People sitting out their lives in the dark saw a huge light; Sitting in that dark, dark country of death, they watched the sun come up.
MSG.17: This Isaiah-prophesied sermon came to life in Galilee the moment Jesus started preaching. He picked up where John left off: "Change your life. God's kingdom is here."
MSG.18: Walking along the beach of Lake Galilee, Jesus saw two brothers: Simon (later called Peter) and Andrew. They were fishing, throwing their nets into the lake. It was their regular work.
MSG.19: Jesus said to them, "Come with me. I'll make a new kind of fisherman out of you. I'll show you how to catch men and women instead of perch and bass."
MSG.20: They didn't ask questions, but simply dropped their nets and followed.
MSG.21: A short distance down the beach they came upon another pair of brothers, James and John, Zebedee's sons. These two were sitting in a boat with their father, Zebedee, mending their fishnets. Jesus made the same offer to them,
MSG.22: and they were just as quick to follow, abandoning boat and father.
MSG.23: From there he went all over Galilee. He used synagogues for meeting places and taught people the truth of God. God's kingdom was his theme—that beginning right now they were under God's government, a good government! He also healed people of their diseases and of the bad effects of their bad lives.
MSG.24: Word got around the entire Roman province of Syria. People brought anybody with an ailment, whether mental, emotional, or physical. Jesus healed them, one and all.
MSG.25: More and more people came, the momentum gathering. Besides those from Galilee, crowds came from the "Ten Towns" across the lake, others up from Jerusalem and Judea, still others from across the Jordan.

NIV.1:
The Temptation of Jesus
Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the desert to be tempted by the devil.
NIV.2: After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry.
NIV.3: The tempter came to him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.”
NIV.4: Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man does not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’”
NIV.5: Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple.
NIV.6:
“If you are the Son of God,” he said, “throw yourself down. For it is written:
“‘He will command his angels concerning you,
and they will lift you up in their hands,
so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’”
NIV.7: Jesus answered him, “It is also written: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’”
NIV.8: Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor.
NIV.9: “All this I will give you,” he said, “if you will bow down and worship me.”
NIV.10: Jesus said to him, “Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’”
NIV.11: Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.
NIV.12:
Jesus Begins to Preach
When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he returned to Galilee.
NIV.13: Leaving Nazareth, he went and lived in Capernaum, which was by the lake in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali—
NIV.14: to fulfill what was said through the prophet Isaiah:
NIV.15:
“Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali,
the way to the sea, along the Jordan,
Galilee of the Gentiles—
NIV.16:
the people living in darkness
have seen a great light;
on those living in the land of the shadow of death
a light has dawned.”
NIV.17: From that time on Jesus began to preach, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is near.”
NIV.18:
The Calling of the First Disciples
As Jesus was walking beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon called Peter and his brother Andrew. They were casting a net into the lake, for they were fishermen.
NIV.19: “Come, follow me,” Jesus said, “and I will make you fishers of men.”
NIV.20: At once they left their nets and followed him.
NIV.21: Going on from there, he saw two other brothers, James son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with their father Zebedee, preparing their nets. Jesus called them,
NIV.22: and immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
NIV.23:
Jesus Heals the Sick
Jesus went throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and sickness among the people.
NIV.24: News about him spread all over Syria, and people brought to him all who were ill with various diseases, those suffering severe pain, the demon-possessed, those having seizures, and the paralyzed, and he healed them.
NIV.25: Large crowds from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea and the region across the Jordan followed him.

NRSVCE.1: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
NRSVCE.2: He fasted forty days and forty nights, and afterwards he was famished.
NRSVCE.3: The tempter came and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command these stones to become loaves of bread.”
NRSVCE.4:
But he answered, “It is written, ‘One does not live by bread alone,
but by every word that comes from the mouth of God.’”
NRSVCE.5: Then the devil took him to the holy city and placed him on the pinnacle of the temple,
NRSVCE.6:
saying to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down; for it is written, ‘He will command his angels concerning you,’
and ‘On their hands they will bear you up,
so that you will not dash your foot against a stone.’”
NRSVCE.7: Jesus said to him, “Again it is written, ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’”
NRSVCE.8: Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor;
NRSVCE.9: and he said to him, “All these I will give you, if you will fall down and worship me.”
NRSVCE.10:
Jesus said to him, “Away with you, Satan! for it is written, ‘Worship the Lord your God,
and serve only him.’”
NRSVCE.11: Then the devil left him, and suddenly angels came and waited on him.
NRSVCE.12: Now when Jesus heard that John had been arrested, he withdrew to Galilee.
NRSVCE.13: He left Nazareth and made his home in Capernaum by the sea, in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali,
NRSVCE.14: so that what had been spoken through the prophet Isaiah might be fulfilled:
NRSVCE.15:
“Land of Zebulun, land of Naphtali,
on the road by the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles —
NRSVCE.16:
the people who sat in darkness
have seen a great light,
and for those who sat in the region and shadow of death
light has dawned.”
NRSVCE.17: From that time Jesus began to proclaim, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.”
NRSVCE.18: As he walked by the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea — for they were fishermen.
NRSVCE.19: And he said to them, “Follow me, and I will make you fish for people.”
NRSVCE.20: Immediately they left their nets and followed him.
NRSVCE.21: As he went from there, he saw two other brothers, James son of Zebedee and his brother John, in the boat with their father Zebedee, mending their nets, and he called them.
NRSVCE.22: Immediately they left the boat and their father, and followed him.
NRSVCE.23: Jesus went throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom and curing every disease and every sickness among the people.
NRSVCE.24: So his fame spread throughout all Syria, and they brought to him all the sick, those who were afflicted with various diseases and pains, demoniacs, epileptics, and paralytics, and he cured them.
NRSVCE.25: And great crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from beyond the Jordan.

NET.1: Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
NET.2: After he fasted forty days and forty nights he was famished.
NET.3: The tempter came and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command these stones to become bread.”
NET.4: But he answered, “It is written, ‘Man does not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God.’”
NET.5: Then the devil took him to the holy city, had him stand on the highest point of the temple,
NET.6: and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down. For it is written, ‘He will command his angels concerning you’ and ‘with their hands they will lift you up, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’”
NET.7: Jesus said to him, “Once again it is written: ‘You are not to put the Lord your God to the test.’”
NET.8: Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain, and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their grandeur.
NET.9: And he said to him, “I will give you all these things if you throw yourself to the ground and worship me.”
NET.10: Then Jesus said to him, “Go away, Satan! For it is written: ‘You are to worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’”
NET.11: Then the devil left him, and angels came and began ministering to his needs.
NET.12: Now when Jesus heard that John had been imprisoned, he went into Galilee.
NET.13: While in Galilee, he moved from Nazareth to make his home in Capernaum by the sea, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali,
NET.14: so that what was spoken by Isaiah the prophet would be fulfilled:
NET.15: “Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, the way by the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles —
NET.16: the people who sit in darkness have seen a great light, and on those who sit in the region and shadow of death a light has dawned.”
NET.17: From that time Jesus began to preach this message: “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is near.”
NET.18: As he was walking by the Sea of Galilee he saw two brothers, Simon (called Peter) and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea (for they were fishermen).
NET.19: He said to them, “Follow me, and I will turn you into fishers of people.”
NET.20: They left their nets immediately and followed him.
NET.21: Going on from there he saw two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, in a boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets. Then he called them.
NET.22: They immediately left the boat and their father and followed him.
NET.23: Jesus went throughout all of Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing all kinds of disease and sickness among the people.
NET.24: So a report about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all who suffered with various illnesses and afflictions, those who had seizures, paralytics, and those possessed by demons, and he healed them.
NET.25: And large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and beyond the Jordan River.
